page.content: In the analysis of dietary habits and mental health, individuals with a PHQ‐9 score of less than 10 exhibited a general diet mean of 4.41 ± 0.05, while those with a score of 10 or greater had a mean of 3.97 ± 0.20, with a p-value of 0.033. For fruit and vegetables, the mean was 3.84 ± 0.06 for the lower score group and 3.51 ± 0.21 for the higher score group (p-value 0.133). High-fat food consumption averaged 2.41 ± 0.05 for the former and 2.16 ± 0.19 for the latter (p-value 0.211). Self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG) showed means of 2.23 ± 0.05 and 2.32 ± 0.18 (p-value 0.659). Exercise means were 3.90 ± 0.06 and 3.31 ± 0.20, respectively, with a significant p-value of 0.005. Foot care averaged 3.97 ± 0.05 versus 3.57 ± 0.17 (p-value 0.022), while bringing along candy showed means of 2.60 ± 0.07 and 2.80 ± 0.25 (p-value 0.239). In a separate study of plant characteristics, WT had an average leaf area of 47.55 ± 1.98 mm², an average cell area of 3160 ± 30 μm², and an average of 15047 ± 586 cells per leaf. Meanwhile, D5aA had 63.92 ± 0.84 mm² leaf area, 3540 ± 159 μm² cell area, and 18129 ± 845 cells per leaf; D4I had 62.0 ± 1.4 mm² leaf area, 3575 ± 113 μm² cell area, and 17392 ± 831 cells per leaf; F6bA had 59.97 ± 3.13 mm² leaf area, 3592 ± 173 μm² cell area, and 16858 ± 885 cells per leaf.

High-fat food consumption averaged 2.41 ± 0.05 for the former and 2.16 ± 0.19 for the latter (p-value 0.211). Self-monitoring
